description: No <meta name="description"> tag found. Search engines will auto-generate a snippet which may be inaccurate.
Title tag exists but is generic ("Sample Page"). Should be descriptive and include target keywords (50-60 chars recommended).
H1 is present and used once. dashboard.tsx uses h1 > h2 correctly. Card.tsx uses h3 appropriately.
No JSON-LD or other structured data markup found. This limits rich snippet eligibility in SERPs.
No <link rel="canonical"> tag present. Risk of duplicate content issues.
No Open Graph (og:title, og:description, og:image) or Twitter Card meta tags. Social sharing previews will be poor.
is missing the alt attribute entirely. This hurts accessibility and image SEO.
Good use of <main>, <nav>, <section> elements. Heading hierarchy is well-structured.
Links use descriptive anchor text ("About Us", "latest deals") and use HTTPS.
No <meta name="robots"> tag or sitemap reference found. Search engine crawling behavior is undefined.
lang="en" is set on <html>, which is good. No hreflang tags, but may not be needed if single-language site.
External stylesheet <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.example.com/styles.css"> is render-blocking. Should use media query, preload, or async loading strategy.
@font-face for "CustomFont" is missing font-display: swap. This causes invisible text (FOIT) during font loading.
is missing width and height attributes, causing Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).
in dashboard is also missing explicit width/height attributes causing CLS risk.
Below-fold image lacks loading="lazy" attribute. All non-critical images should be lazily loaded.
Reading offsetHeight then immediately writing style.minHeight causes forced synchronous reflow (layout thrashing). Use requestAnimationFrame or CSS-based approach.
setTimeout inserts content (setItems) without pre-allocated space, causing layout shift. Reserve space with min-height or skeleton UI.
Inline <script> in body is parser-blocking. Consider defer/async pattern or move to external file with defer attribute.
console.log left in production code. Adds minor overhead and leaks internal info. Remove or gate behind development flag.
document.querySelector('.card-highlight') in onClick handler bypasses React's virtual DOM. Use useRef instead for better performance and safety.
id="card-item" is hardcoded. When Card is rendered in a list, all instances share the same ID, violating HTML spec and breaking querySelector/getElementById.
List items rendered via items.map() lack a key prop. React cannot efficiently reconcile the list, leading to unnecessary re-renders.
No <link rel="preconnect"> for cdn.example.com or font origin. Adding preconnect would reduce connection setup latency.
No inline critical CSS. All styles depend on external stylesheet which is render-blocking. Consider inlining critical-path CSS.
Images use .png format. Consider modern formats (WebP, AVIF) for smaller file sizes and faster loading.
Inline script accesses DOM directly. If moved to external file it should use defer. Current inline placement blocks parsing.
innerHTML is set with unsanitized user input (location.hash). This is both a security vulnerability and forces the browser to re-parse the DOM subtree.
External CDN resource (cdn.example.com/styles.css) introduces a single point of failure and additional DNS/TCP/TLS overhead. Consider self-hosting critical assets.
